name: US Manufacturing Productivity and Production Worker Real Compensation

book_quotes.quote: The paths of indexes of manufacturing productivity (output per worker hour) and real employee compensation, as depicted in figure 2.5, bring new considerations to the fore. Productivity growth is essentially a measure of technical change, and its steady long-term rise speaks to the fundamental role of technological progress in capitalist development.

Manufacturing productivity (yr) for 1860–1970 (1958 = 100) from BEA (1966, Series A173) and for 1950–2009 from BLS International Data, http://www.bls.gov/fls/#productivity, Table 1: Output per Hour in Manufacturing, 19 Countries or Areas (2007 = 100). Both series rebased to 1889 = 100 and spliced in 1950 with the earlier series rescaled to match in 1950. Production worker real compensation for 1774–2010 based on manufacturing production worker nominal compensation (ec) in $/hr and Consumer Price Index (CPI) for 1774–2010 from Measuring Worth.com, with sources and methods of the latter described in “Characteristics of the Production-Worker Compensation Series” and “What Was the Consumer Price Index Then? A Data Study,” respectively. Real compensation was derived as ec/CPI.

primary_source.agency: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis + U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics + MeasuringWorth
primary_source.publication: BEA (1966) Long Term Economic Growth Series A173; BLS International Labor Comparisons (Output per Hour in Manufacturing); MeasuringWorth Production-Worker Compensation and CPI
primary_source.table_or_series_id: BEA LTEG A173 (1860–1970) + BLS FLS Table 1 (1950–2009); MeasuringWorth ec & CPI (1774–2010)
primary_source.license: Public domain (US govt) + MeasuringWorth academic license
primary_source.replaced_by: BLS International Comparisons program was discontinued in 2013; FRED OPHMFG is the modern productivity successor.

formula: real_compensation = nominal_compensation_per_hour / CPI; productivity = spliced(BEA_A173, BLS_FLS_Table1)

components.label: BEA (1966) LTEG Series A173 (Mfg output per man-hour)
BLS International Comparisons Table 1 (Output per Hour in Mfg, 19 countries)

extension_candidates.subseries_label: FRED OPHMFG (Manufacturing Sector: Real Output Per Hour of All Persons)
concerns: BLS discontinued the FLS International Comparisons program in 2013; OPHMFG is the US-only manufacturing-sector continuation.
Same source as original; direct continuation.

methodology_notes: Two co-plotted series: productivity (spliced BEA + BLS) and real compensation (derived as ec/CPI).
CD2 dossier S007 ported and validated. Note: CD2 known issue flagged sparse BEA coverage 1860–1889 — plot starts at 1889.
BLS FLS program was sunset in 2013; future extensions of productivity must transition to OPHMFG (US-only) per Appendix 2.1 fidelity.
open_questions: BLS International Labor Comparisons URL (http://www.bls.gov/fls/) returns 404 in 2026; legacy data is archived at https://www.bls.gov/data/#productivity but the original table is no longer maintained. Flag for Phase 4.
